scale_note: Scores are 1-10 relative ratings based on public source consensus (sailorpiece.org, Beebom, ProGameGuides, PocketGamer) plus update recency. They are directional, not exact in-game DPS numbers. Higher is better.

Shadow Monarch.verdict: The safest all-around best sword: top-tier damage, wide AoE and strong iframes make it forgiving in every mode.

Rimuru.verdict: Highest sustained DPS in the game — the boss-melt king. Slightly less PvP utility than Shadow Monarch.

True Manipulator.verdict: The clearest PvP control pick: crowd control, iframes and disruption beat raw damage in duels.

Yamato.verdict: Versatile damage with good mobility — a strong do-everything S-tier without a glaring weakness.

Sin of Pride.verdict: Top-3 single-target damage. Pick it for heavy boss fights where AoE matters less than burst.

Atomic.verdict: Devastating AoE with short cooldowns — best for raids and dense wave clearing.

Abyssal Empress.verdict: A PvP hybrid: control plus damage, strong when you need disruption with decent damage uptime.

Dragon Goddess.verdict: Highest base damage in the game, scales further with the Dragon Queen title — but needs build support.

Great Mage.verdict: The top magic-scaling sword — excellent for magic builds and balanced across modes.

Ice Queen.verdict: Freeze and slow control — best when you need to lock down mobile bosses or PvP targets.

Anti Magic.verdict: A hard counter to magic builds in PvP. Situational but devastating against the right opponent.

Ragna.verdict: A solid mid-game sword that progresses you while you save resources for an S-tier upgrade.

Shadow.verdict: Strong synergy with the Shadowborn race for stealth-damage builds.

Ichigo.verdict: Reliable sustained DPS — a dependable mid-game pick that holds up well into late game.

Aizen.verdict: A versatile magic-plus-sword hybrid. High community search demand and good flexibility.

Jinwoo.verdict: Acceptable damage for Solo Leveling fans, but outshone by A and S tier in raids.

Katana.verdict: An entry-level starter sword — replace it as soon as you can clear a boss for a better blade.

Dark Blade.verdict: Outdated early-game kit. Fine for the first islands, weak everywhere after.

Saber.verdict: A beginner-friendly Boss Island sword — a stepping stone toward Qin Shi and Ichigo routes.
